learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an enhanced understanding of your preferred field of study and potential career pathways through reflective engagement in either an individual or group-based professional placement.

Apply theoretical knowledge to real-world contexts by undertaking tasks and responsibilities—individually or collaboratively—that develop practical skills and professional experience in a relevant workplace setting.
Critically evaluate your own capabilities and contributions, as well as those of your team (where applicable), in the process of applying knowledge and skills within a professional environment.
Demonstrate the ability to engage with workplace practices, policies, and professional standards, both independently and as part of a team, in alignment with expectations of the field of study.

assessments.task_description:
This is an individual assessment. The Learning Contract is to be completed during the first week of placement. Students will define their Placement goals, list learning objectives and the activities that will enable them to achieve these learning objectives, and then align the objectives to an employability literacy (refer to Employability Framework).

The Risk Assessment is to be completed during the first week of placement. Students will identify hazards associated with placement activities and discuss any required risk management/training with their Supervisor.

Templates for the Learning Contract, Employability Framework and Risk Assessment are available on Blackboard under Assessment. Both the Learning Contract and Risk Assessment must be endorsed by the Placement Supervisor prior to submission.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, in Blackboard, via the submission link for the assessment by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: See hurdle requirements wording. This is a pass-fail course and to pass this assessment and the course students must submit and pass this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Reflective Practice 1 - due after the first block of 40 hours

Reflective Practice 2 - due after the second block of 40 hours

Reflective Practice 3 - due after the third block of 40 hours

Progressive reflections due during placement.

Students will calculate their own schedule for submitting Reflective Practice assessments.

Reflective Practice 3 must be submitted by the end of the placement.
assessments.task_description:
This is an individual assessment. Using the SEAL process for self reflection and the Employability Framework provided in Blackboard, students will periodically reflect on events and activities that occur during the placement. Students are to submit three reflections during the placement, evenly spaced over the 120 hours. Further instructions on the format are available on Blackboard under the Assessment tab.

  • Reflective Practice 1 is for situations that occur in the first block of 40 hours of placement.
  • Reflective Practice 2 is for situations that occur in the second block of 40 hours of placement.
  • Reflective Practice 3 is for situations that occur in the final block of 40 hours of placement.

For example, if the placement period is six weeks, the first reflection will be submitted after two weeks of placement are completed, the second reflection after four weeks, and the third upon completion of the placement.

Students will calculate their own schedule for submitting Reflective Practice assessments. Reflective Practice 3 must be submitted by the end of the placement.

Training on using the SEAL process for self-reflection will be given in the Pre-placement workshop.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, in Blackboard, via the Turnitin link by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: See hurdle wording. This is a pass-fail course and to pass this assessment and the course, students must submit and pass all 3 of the reflective practices.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is an individual assessment. Students will deliver a three-minute-thesis style presentation, using a single PowerPoint slide, to share details of their experience with the rest of the cohort. The presentation should be no longer than 3-4 minutes and describe what you did (activities), the impact your activities had on you, and what you learnt. To avoid going overtime, you can focus on 2-3 activities that had the greatest impact on your learning/skills development.

The presentation should be written for a general audience (i.e., not too technical) and convey details of the activities undertaken during placement without breaching any confidentiality restrictions of the host organisation.

All students will deliver their presentation live via Zoom during one of the post-placement seminars regardless of whether the placement hours have been partially or fully completed. Note: if your placement dates clash with this, you must seek 'time off' from your placement.

All students must attend the first 1-hour session (directly after the careers part of post-placement workshop) and be ready to present. A second session is scheduled on the day as an overflow if needed. For identity verification, students must turn on their camera and show their face the entire time to receive a grade.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd> Pass/Fail. Student must achieve at least 8/20 for a Pass. </dd>
assessments.task_description: This is an individual assessment. Prior to completion of your placement, UQ will send a request to your placement supervisor to complete an assessment of your performance in the workplace and to endorse the hours you claimed to have worked; students must keep a diary of their hours and activities and provide this to their supervisor at completion of the placement. Supervisors will submit this report at the completion of your placement directly to UQ. Supervisors will be encouraged to use the report as an opportunity to provide formal feedback to you. The feedback will be released to students following review by UQ.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Placement supervisor to submit report directly to UQ as instructed in the email.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Placement

Students will undertake a minimum of 120 hours (recommended maximum 160 hours) placement in an approved workplace between these dates. Note: if placement dates clash with workshop dates, students must seek 'time off' from their placement to attend compulsory workshops.

Students must keep a diary of their hours and activities and provide this to their supervisor at completion of the placement.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
